I INTRODUCTION 
I.1 Product name  
GLOBAL_CLIMATE-VARIABILITY_NINO34_SST_anom  
I.2 Indicator evaluation method 
NINO34 sea surface temperature anomaly (°C)  is defined as the difference between the sea 
surface temperature averaged over an area between 170°W -120°W and 5°S -5°N and the 
climatological reference value over same area. Spatial averaging was weighted by surface 
area. Monthly mean values are given here. The reference period is 1993-2014. 
 
Figure 1: Monthly index computed using GREP ensemble mean (solid line) and spread based on the standard 
deviation between members (bars) of sea surface temperature anomalies relative to the 1993-2014 climatology, 
and area averaged over the NINO box 3.4 (170°W-120°W, 5°S-5°N). This indicator was computed using the GREP 
multiproduct global reanalysis (GLOBAL_MULTIYEAR_PHY_ENS_001_031) over the 1993-2023 period.

I.3 CMEMS products used as input for the computations 
 
Product name (and Type) Documentation 
GLOBAL_MULTIYEAR_PHY_ENS_001
_031 
QUID:  CMEMS-GLO-QUID-001-031.pdf 
PUM: CMEMS-GLO-PUM-001-031.pdf (copernicus.eu) 
 
The 3 reanalyses of GREP (GLORYS, CGLORS, ORAS5) GLOBAL_MULTIYEAR_PHY_ENS_001_031 
use the NEMO ocean model with assimilation of in situ and satellite observations.
